title: toxicodendron excisum
intro: Toxicodendron excisum, also known as the poison sumac plant, is a plant species native to North America, belonging to the Anacardiaceae family, which is known for its toxic sap. The medicinal properties of Toxicodendron excisum include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as arthritis and eczema. Toxicodendron excisum is often used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are applied topically to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
